settings.companyNameHint: The display name for your company.
settings.descriptionHint: Optional description shown in the company profile.
settings.brandColorHint: Sets the hue for the company icon. Leave empty for auto-generated color.
settings.requireApproval: Require board approval for new hires
settings.requireApprovalHint: New agent hires stay pending until approved by board.
settings.inviteHint: Generate a link to invite humans or agents to this company.
settings.inviteLinkExpiry: Invite links expire after 72 hours and allow both human and agent joins.
settings.archiveDescription: Archive this company to hide it from the sidebar. This persists in the database.
settings.archiveConfirm: Archive company "{{name}}"? It will be hidden from the sidebar.
settings.noCompanySelected: No company selected. Select a company from the switcher above.

pathInstructions.browserSecurity: Browser security blocks apps from reading full local paths via a file picker. Copy the absolute path and paste it into the input.
pathInstructions.pasteIntoInput: Paste the result into the path input.
pathInstructions.findFolderExplorer: Find the folder in File Explorer.
pathInstructions.copyOutputPaste: Copy the output and paste it into the path input.
pathInstructions.fullPathTitle: How to get a full path

help.name: Display name for this agent.
help.title: Job title shown in the org chart.
help.role: Organizational role. Determines position and capabilities.
help.reportsTo: The agent this one reports to in the org hierarchy.
help.capabilities: Describes what this agent can do. Shown in the org chart and used for task routing.
help.adapterType: Which adapter runs this agent, such as a local CLI, generic process, or HTTP webhook.
help.cwd: Default working directory for local adapters. Use an absolute path on the machine running Baton.
help.promptTemplate: Prompt sent on each heartbeat. Supports variables like {{ agent.id }}, {{ agent.name }}, and {{ agent.role }}.
help.model: Override the default model for this adapter when supported.
help.thinkingEffort: Controls model reasoning depth. Available values depend on adapter and model.
help.chrome: Passes --chrome so Claude Code can use its Chrome integration.
help.dangerouslySkipPermissions: Runs Claude Code without permission prompts. Useful for unattended agents.
help.dangerouslyBypassSandbox: Runs Codex without sandbox restrictions so it can access the full filesystem and network.
help.search: Lets Codex use web search during runs when the adapter supports it.
help.maxTurnsPerRun: Maximum number of agentic turns or tool calls a single run can make.
help.command: Executable to run, such as node or python.
help.localCommand: Override the exact local CLI path Baton should invoke, such as /usr/local/bin/claude or codex.
help.args: Command-line arguments, entered as a comma-separated list.
help.extraArgs: Additional CLI arguments for local adapters, entered as a comma-separated list.
help.envVars: Environment variables injected into the adapter process. Use plain values or secret references.
help.webhookUrl: Endpoint that receives POST requests when Baton wakes this agent.
help.heartbeatInterval: Runs this agent automatically on a timer instead of only when manually triggered or assigned work.
help.timeoutSec: Maximum run duration in seconds. Use 0 for no timeout.
help.graceSec: Seconds to wait after interrupting before force-killing the process.
help.wakeOnDemand: Allows assignments, UI actions, API calls, and automations to wake this agent immediately.
help.cooldownSec: Minimum seconds Baton waits before starting another run for this agent.
help.maxConcurrentRuns: Maximum number of runs that may execute at the same time for this agent.
help.budgetMonthlyCents: Monthly spending limit in cents. Use 0 for an unlimited budget.
help.bootstrapPrompt: Optional first-run setup prompt sent before the agent starts its normal task flow.
help.instructionsFilePath: Absolute path to a markdown instructions file such as AGENTS.md. Baton injects it into the runtime prompt.
help.issuePrefix: Prefix Baton uses when generating issue identifiers such as NMD-42.
help.locale: Default language for the board UI and the text Baton agents generate for this company.
help.requireBoardApprovalForNewAgents: Keeps new hires pending until the board reviews and approves them.
help.leadAgentId: Primary lead for the project. Use this to signal who owns coordination, not issue assignment.
help.projectStatus: Current project lifecycle state such as backlog, planned, in progress, completed, or cancelled.
help.targetDate: Optional target date the team is aiming for. It helps communicate schedule expectations.
help.workspaceTooltip: Workspaces tell agents where code or repos for this project live.

projectHelp.workspaceDescription: Choose the mode that matches how this project is stored so agents know whether to work from a local folder, a GitHub repo, or both.

local.name: local
local.value: Only a local folder path is tracked. Best when the work already exists on this machine.

repo.name: repo
repo.value: Only a GitHub repository URL is tracked. Best when the repo exists remotely but no local checkout is pinned yet.

both.name: both
both.value: Store both the local path and the repo URL so agents can use the checked-out folder and still know the remote source.

adapter.title: Adapter setup: {{adapter}}
adapter.summary: Choose how Baton wakes this agent, then fill in only the fields that matter for that adapter.
adapter.localNote: For local adapters, set the working directory to the repo root and only override the command when Baton cannot find the CLI on its own.
adapter.remoteNote: Remote adapters are best when the runtime already exists outside Baton and you only need Baton to trigger it.

claude_local.description: Claude local runs Claude Code directly on this machine and works well for hands-on engineering agents.
claude_local.recommendation: Recommended when you want a Baton-managed coding session with a real local checkout.

codex_local.description: Codex local runs the Codex CLI on this machine and is a strong default for autonomous coding loops.
codex_local.recommendation: Recommended for trusted local environments where the agent needs full repo access and fast iteration.

opencode_local.description: OpenCode local uses the OpenCode CLI for local execution with Baton-managed prompts and heartbeats.
opencode_local.recommendation: Recommended when your team already standardizes on OpenCode models or workflows.

cursor.description: Cursor local wakes a local Cursor agent session from Baton while keeping the task flow in the control plane.
cursor.recommendation: Recommended when the operator already works primarily in Cursor and wants Baton orchestration on top.

process.description: Process adapters run an arbitrary command plus args, which is useful for custom scripts or wrappers.
process.recommendation: Recommended when you already have a script, binary, or shim that should stay as the execution entrypoint.

http.description: HTTP adapters call a webhook instead of starting a local process.
http.recommendation: Recommended when the agent is hosted elsewhere and Baton only needs to signal it to wake up.

workspace.title: Workspace setup guide
workspace.summary: Workspaces tell agents where code lives. Pick the smallest combination that still points the agent at the right source of truth.
workspace.recommendation: Use both local path and repo URL when you have a checked-out folder and want agents to understand the linked remote repository too.

onboarding.nameCompanyDesc: This is the organization your agents will work for.
onboarding.learnBatonDesc: See how the core entities connect before you create your company and first agent.
onboarding.missionPlaceholder: What is this company trying to achieve?
onboarding.createAgentDesc: Choose how this agent will run tasks.
onboarding.workingDirectoryHint: Baton works best if you create a new folder for your agents to keep their memories and stay organized. Create a new folder and put the path here.
onboarding.adapterEnvCheckDesc: Runs a live probe that asks the adapter CLI to respond with hello.
onboarding.giveTaskDesc: Give your agent a small task to start with — a bug fix, a research question, writing a script.
onboarding.descriptionPlaceholder: Add more detail about what the agent should do...
onboarding.readyToLaunchDesc: Everything is set up. Your assigned task already woke the agent, so you can jump straight to the issue.
onboarding.statusFlowSummary: The normal path is backlog -> todo -> in_progress -> done. Use blocked when something external stops progress.

company.title: Company
company.description: The top-level container. Budgets, language, agents, projects, and issues all live under the company.

project.title: Project
project.description: A focused work area that groups issues and points agents at the right workspace or repository.

agent.title: Agent
agent.description: An AI teammate with a role, reporting line, adapter configuration, and heartbeat schedule.

issue.title: Issue
issue.description: The concrete unit of work an agent checks out, executes, comments on, and moves through statuses.

backlog.label: Backlog
backlog.description: Use this for work that matters but is not ready to start yet.

todo.label: Todo
todo.description: The task is ready and can be picked up by the assigned agent.

inProgress.label: In Progress
inProgress.description: The issue has been checked out and one agent is actively working on it.

done.label: Done
done.description: The requested work is complete and the outcome has been reported back in comments.

blocked.label: Blocked
blocked.description: Use this when progress stopped because someone else, missing context, or an external dependency must unblock it.

onboarding.defaultTaskDescription:
Setup yourself as the CEO. Use the ceo persona found here: [https://github.com/atototo/baton/blob/main/companies/default/ceo/AGENTS.md](https://github.com/atototo/baton/blob/main/companies/default/ceo/AGENTS.md)

Ensure you have a folder agents/ceo and then download this AGENTS.md as well as the sibling HEARTBEAT.md, SOUL.md, and TOOLS.md. and set that AGENTS.md as the path to your agents instruction file

And after you've finished that, hire yourself a Founding Engineer agent
onboarding.anthropicKeyWarning: Claude failed while ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is set. You can clear it in this CEO adapter config and retry the probe.

errors.selectCompanyBeforeEnvTest: Create or select a company before testing adapter environment.
errors.unsetAnthropicRetryStillFailing: Retried with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY unset in adapter config, but the environment test is still failing.
errors.unsetAnthropicRetryFailed: Failed to unset ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and retry.

approval.name: Name
approval.title: Title
approval.confirmedDesc: Requesting agent was notified to review this approval and linked issues.
approval.linkedIssuesNote: Linked issues remain open until the requesting agent follows up and closes them.
approval.deleteConfirm: Delete this disapproved agent? This cannot be undone.
